event_text: Genghis Khan unites the Mongol tribes and founds the Mongol Empire.
Kublai Khan establishes the Yuan dynasty in China.
The Black Death spreads through Europe, North Africa and western Asia.
Constantinople falls to the Ottoman Empire.
The Gutenberg Bible is produced using movable type printing in Europe.
Christopher Columbus's first voyage reaches the Caribbean.
The Protestant Reformation begins in Europe.
The English East India Company is founded.
The United States Declaration of Independence is adopted.
The French Revolution begins.
Haiti declares independence from France.
Napoleon is defeated at Waterloo.
Queen Victoria's reign coincides with major imperial and industrial change.
The American Civil War is fought.
The Suez Canal opens.
The First World War is fought across Europe, the Middle East, Africa and the oceans.
The influenza pandemic spreads worldwide.
The Wall Street crash helps trigger the Great Depression.
The Second World War is fought across Europe, Asia, Africa and the Pacific.
The United Nations Charter enters into force.
India and Pakistan become independent as British India is partitioned.
The People's Republic of China is proclaimed.
Apollo 11 lands humans on the Moon.
The fall of the Berlin Wall symbolises the end of the Cold War order in Europe.
The Soviet Union dissolves.
The September 11 attacks lead to major international political and security changes.
COVID-19 is declared a pandemic by the World Health Organization.
